items.title: EPF rejects Sunway's IJM takeover offer: Spokesperson
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Employees Provident Fund (EPF) has decided to decline the takeover offer by Sunway Bhd for its shares in IJM Corp Bhd, according to an unnamed spokesperson. EPF, which holds a 20.52 per cent stake in IJM, becomes the second government-linked investment fund to reject Sunway's offer, following Permodalan Nasional Bhd (PNB), which holds 13.3 per cent. PNB said on March 16 that its investment committee had decided not to accept the offer, citing IJM's intrinsic value and potential future gains relative to Sunway's share price.

items.title: A-GC rejects representation, Chinese duo to face trial over Mindef trespass
items.summary: Defence lawyer, Ooi Hong Ming, who represents photographers Li Luorong, 28, and Chen Junhao, 21, said deputy public prosecutor Nor Aisyah Mohamed Zanyuin informed Sessions Court judge Suhaila Haron of the decision during recent proceedings. "The next case management is set for Thursday," he said. On the first charge, Li and Chen are jointly accused of flying a DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise drone to capture aerial footage in a gazetted prohibited area without authorisation with the intent of acquiring official information detrimental to national security.

items.title: Government ready to roll out cloud seeding as prolonged heat grips several states nationwide
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 30 — The Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES) is prepared to implement intervention measures, including cloud seeding, to mitigate the impact of the prolonged hot weather currently affecting several states nationwide. Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said the measures would be carried out based on prevailing needs, guided by data and technical reports from relevant agencies to ensure the impact of the weather phenomenon is effectively managed, particularly in sectors such as agriculture. Arthur said the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) would continue to play a key role in providing accurate weather information to support follow-up actions, while helping relevant agencies and the public make the necessary preparations.

items.title: A maths book, a suspected drunk driver, a father lost
items.summary: Nor Nadia Abdul Majid, 32, said her husband, Amirul Hafiz Omar, 33, had left home at about 10am to go to Section 19 to purchase a Mathematics book for their eldest child. "He said he wanted to buy a Mathematics book for our child, as he had been planning to do so for several days. My heart was shattered," she said in tears.

items.title: Muar student awaiting SPM results dies in crash with bus at Parit Jawa traffic light junction
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — An 18-year-old student awaiting his Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) results died after colliding with a bus at the Parit Jawa traffic light junction in Muar, Johor, yesterday. Muar district police chief, Assistant Commissioner Raiz Mukhliz Azman Aziz, said preliminary investigations indicated the bus, coming from Batu Pahat towards Muar town, had stopped at the traffic lights. “As the lights turned green, the bus driver signalled a left turn when the student’s motorcycle collided with the left side of the bus.

items.title: Police seek charges in Wangsa Maju 'protection money' case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have referred investigation papers involving two men allegedly demanding "protection money" from a massage parlour in Wangsa Maju to the Deputy Public Prosecutor (DPP), recommending that charges be filed. Wangsa Maju police chief Senior Assistant Commissioner Mohamad Lazim Ismail said the papers were submitted today. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Lorry driver in Batu Pahat pleads not guilty to slapping, punching and kicking wife for leaving home without permission
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — A 39-year-old lorry driver pleaded not guilty at the Magistrates’ Court in Batu Pahat, Johor, today to charges of assaulting his 40-year-old wife after she left their home without his knowledge. Mohamed Hamka Salleh, father of six, entered the plea before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass, according to Harian Metro . Fearful of further harm, the victim lodged a police report at the Batu Pahat District Police Headquarters.

items.title: Give Malaysia’s tour buses 3,000 litres diesel subsidy, travel industry urges govt amid soaring prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The Malaysian government should offer diesel subsidies to tour buses and tour vans amid an 82 per cent jump in local retail diesel prices due to the Iran war, Malaysian Inbound Tourism Association (MITA) urged today. MITA president Mint Leong said local tourism transport operators are struggling to cope with and absorb the increased costs from the diesel price hike, as most of their vehicles such as tour buses and tour vans use diesel. MITA vice-president (transport) Logeswaran K. Sukumar urged the government to not abandon the local tourism players whom he said are the ‘backbone’ of tourism in Malaysia.

items.title: Over 50,000 devotees flock to Maran temple for Panguni Uthiram, police tighten safety measures
items.summary: KUANTAN, March 30 — Over 50,000 Hindu devotees, both local and international, are expected to visit the Sri Maranthandavar Temple in Sungai Jerik, Maran, for the Panguni Uthiram festival, which runs from today until Wednesday. Maran police chief Supt Wong Kim Wai said an estimated 10,000 vehicles are expected in the area during the period. The public is advised to use alternative routes via Jalan Simpang Bangas, continuing to Jalan Utama and Bandar Jengka.

items.title: Police confirm Victor Chin detained in Ops Viking, RM473m assets seized in money‑laundering crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Police have confirmed that businessman Victor Chin Boon Loong was among 10 individuals detained on suspicion of involvement in money laundering activities under Ops Viking, which was conducted between October 15 last year and January 13 this year. Bukit Aman AMLA (Anti-Money Laundering and Anti-Terrorism Financing) Investigation Unit chief Datuk Fazlisyam Abd Majid said Victor Chin was detained in December last year and has since been released on police bail, although he may be recalled if necessary. He said the operation was launched on October 14 last year based on intelligence on money laundering activities dating back to 2018, with a formal investigation opened on Oct 25 under Section 4(1) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (Act 613).

items.title: 1,085 Kada padi farmers affected by drought across six districts
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A total of 1,085 paddy farmers under Kemubu Agricultural Development Authority (Kada) are affected by the ongoing drought in several major districts in Kelantan. Kada chairman Khalid Abdul Samad said the affected areas involved six districts, namely Kota Baru Utara (KBU), Kota Baru Selatan (KBS), Pasir Puteh, Bachok, Pasir Mas and Tumpat. He said the total area affected covers 3,638 hectares, involving various levels of impact on paddy crops.

items.title: Thunderstorm warning for KL, 12 states until 6pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning at 3.30pm today for Kuala Lumpur and 12 states, with heavy rain and strong winds expected until 6pm. In the east coast, several districts in Kelantan, Terengganu and Pahang are also expected to experience adverse weather, including Jeli, Kuala Krai, Gua Musang, Besut, Hulu Terengganu, Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub and Bentong. In the Klang Valley, the advisory includes Selangor's Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat districts, as well as Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Malaysia’s tourism industry announces 70-80pc maximum price hike for tour buses, vans due to diesel pinch
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Tour bus and tour van operators will be able to increase their prices by up to 80 per cent, the Malaysian Inbound Tourism Association (MITA) announced today in a new price guideline effective from today. “Therefore with that, we announce today price increase for tour buses and tour vans. Leong however said this is only a guide for the “ceiling price” or maximum price that tour bus and tour van operators can charge, and said this announcement is made with the aim of price transparency for the entire tourism industry and for tour agencies abroad and tourists globally.

items.title: Transport Ministry comes down hard: No compromise on drivers under influence after fatal Klang crash; prison, possible compensation for victims’ families
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 30 — The government will not compromise on any individual driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said, following a fatal crash in Klang involving a motorcyclist. Loke also noted that amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987, effective October 2020, provide for heavier penalties, including 10 to 15 years’ jail and fines of RM50,000 to RM100,000 for causing death while driving under the influence, with even stiffer sentences for repeat offenders. The ministry is also considering further amendments to require offenders to compensate victims’ families, in addition to serving prison sentences.

items.title: High‑stakes chase in Wang Kelian: Perlis police nab five, seize 510kg meth worth RM25.5m
items.summary: KANGAR, March 30 — Police arrested five individuals, including a married couple, and seized 510 kilogrammes (kg) of methamphetamine worth an estimated RM25.5 million in three separate raids in Wang Kelian, Kaki Bukit near Padang Besar last week. Hussein said police arrested the married couple and a male suspect from two cars they were travelling in along the main road in Wang Kelian during the first raid on March 24. Hussein said the arrest of the three individuals led to the capture of two more male suspects in two separate raids around Wang Kelian on March 27 and 28.

items.title: Court rules Papagomo guilty of sedition, RM4,000 fine imposed for post against King
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Blogger Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, better known as Papagomo, was fined RM4,000 in default four months’ jail by the Sessions Court here today after being found guilty of publishing seditious content deemed to incite hatred against the King, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, two years ago. On May 2, 2024, the former Umno Youth executive council member claimed trial for publishing seditious material on his Twitter (now X) account in relation to an issue involving the owner of KK Supermart & Superstore Sdn Bhd meeting Sultan Ibrahim. Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Mohd Sabri Othman urged the court to impose a commensurate sentence, citing the accused’s background as a former police officer who should have had a better understanding of the law.

items.title: Tamparuli river getting drier after no rain for almost a month
items.summary: TAMPARULI: The water level of the Tamparuli River, which is the main source for two Telibong Water Treatment Plants, has dropped significantly due to hot weather and the absence of rain in this district for nearly a month. Tamparuli State Assemblyman, Datuk Seri Wilfred Madius Tangau, said the Telibong Water Treatment Plant should be capable of producing 80 million litres of water per day for consumers in Tuaran and northern Kota Kinabalu. "If the drought continues and affects the water level of the Tamparuli River, water rationing for consumers will be unavoidable," he said today.

items.title: LPP staffer charged with RM141,872 misappropriation of oil palm produce
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A staff member of the Farmers' Organisation Authority (LPP) was charged in the Sessions Court here with 16 counts of dishonestly misappropriating property worth RM141,872.67 belonging to the Kota Tinggi Timur Area Farmers' Organisation. The accused, who also served as the organisation's farm manager, is alleged to have dishonestly converted oil palm fresh fruit bunches valued at RM141,872.67 for his own use. He was charged under Section 403 of the Penal Code, which carries a sentence of between six months and five years' imprisonment, and may also include caning and a fine upon conviction.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm warning, heavy rain and strong winds expected across nine states until evening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning, with heavy rain and strong winds expected across nine states until 5 pm today. In a statement today, MetMalaysia said that the weather is expected in Kedah’s Baling and across multiple districts in Perak, including Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim. The warning also covers Pahang (Cameron Highlands and Bentong), Selangor (Hulu Selangor, Gombak and Hulu Langat), Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah, Rembau and Tampin), Melaka, and Johor (Muar and Tangkak).

items.title: Penang schools beat the heat: Classes go on with safety first
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The state Education Department will continue face-to-face schooling despite the ongoing heatwave, prioritising the health and well-being of students across 398 schools in Penang. He said that engagement sessions had been held with schools and district education offices to ensure preparedness for any sudden changes in weather. "Schools have undertaken several initiatives, including advising parents for students to bring sufficient drinking water, allowing more comfortable attire, and ensuring adequate water supply at canteens.

items.title: AirAsia won’t cancel flights, Tony Fernandes says demand strong as West Asia conflict lifts fuel costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Capital A chief executive officer Tan Sri Tony Fernandes said AirAsia fares may see a modest increase while remaining affordable, following the conflict in West Asia. He said that the low-cost carrier will not cancel flights amid rising crude oil prices, as demand remains strong. However, rising costs should not be borne by airlines alone, but require collective efforts across the industry, said Fernandes.

items.title: Hot weather: Falling water levels at Lake Beris disrupt daily activities
items.summary: SIK: Prolonged hot weather has lowered the level of Lake Beris, affecting daily life and posing challenges for small-scale tourism operators. "If the hot weather continues, activities here, especially those on the water, like kayaking, will definitely be affected," he told Bernama today. He explained that the receding water since January has left a floating café stranded on dry ground.

items.title: Cops arrest five, seize RM25.5mil worth of meth in Perlis
items.summary: KANGAR: Police arrested five individuals, including a married couple, and seized 510 kilogrammes (kg) of methamphetamine worth an estimated RM25.5 million in three separate raids in Wang Kelian, Kaki Bukit near Padang Besar last week. Hussein said police arrested the married couple and a male suspect from two cars they were travelling in along the main road in Wang Kelian during the first raid on March 24. Hussein said the arrest of the three individuals led to the capture of two more male suspects in two separate raids around Wang Kelian on March 27 and 28.

items.title: Steering Malaysia through global shockwaves with collective strength
items.summary: AT a time when global tensions are once again testing the fragility of economic stability, particularly through evolving geopolitical fault lines involving the United States, Israel, and Iran, Malaysia stands at a decisive policy juncture. The central argument is straightforward: targeted subsidies under the government are not merely a fiscal recalibration, but a strategic imperative to safeguard national resilience, preserve social cohesion, and sustain long-term economic credibility in an increasingly uncertain global environment. It unfolds against a backdrop of heightened global energy volatility, persistent supply chain disruptions, and rising geopolitical risk premiums that inevitably translate into domestic inflationary pressures.

items.title: Taiping Clock Tower chimes again after years of silence
items.summary: TAIPING: After years of silence, the iconic Taiping Clock Tower is ringing again, breathing new life into the town's historic centre. State Tourism, Industry, Investment and Corridor Development Committee chairman Loh Sze Yee said the upgrade forms part of a broader effort to strengthen Taiping's tourism infrastructure. "This clock tower has served multiple purposes over the years, including as a fire station and a police station.

items.title: Thunderstorms, strong winds to hit Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to lash parts of Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Sarawak until 4pm today. In Negri Sembilan, the alert covers the districts of Jelebu, Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah and Rembau. Meanwhile, in Sarawak, areas expected to experience adverse weather include Kuching, Serian, Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong and Daro in Mukah.

items.title: Mercy Malaysia backs MACC push for new law on NGO donations, calls for Royal Charities Commission
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Mercy Malaysia has voiced support for a proposal by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) to introduce new legislation on public fund management, including donations collected by non-governmental organisations (NGOs). Its president, Datuk Dr Ahmad Faizal Mohd Perdaus, said the establishment of a Royal Charities Commission would be a timely and significant move towards strengthening accountability and ensuring charitable contributions are managed with integrity and proper oversight. “Mercy Malaysia believes that maintaining public trust is fundamental to the effectiveness of humanitarian work.

items.title: Motorcyclist killed in drink-driving crash was a father working two jobs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A delivery rider killed in a collision with a suspected drink-driver on Jalan Raya Barat yesterday worked two jobs to support his family. Amirul Hafiz Omar, who was in his 30s, worked at a warehouse by day and as a delivery rider by night. During the day, he worked at a warehouse, and at night he took on part-time work as a delivery rider," said his friend and colleague of 11 years, Illyasa Saipuzzaman.

items.title: Capital A names Effendy deputy CEO as it enters next growth phase [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Capital A Bhd has appointed Effendy Shahul Hamid as deputy chief executive officer, effective April 6, reinforcing the company's leadership bench as it enters its next phase of growth. Following the disposal of its aviation businesses to AirAsia X Bhd in January, Capital A is now focused on growing and scaling its other core businesses, namely Asia Digital Engineering, Teleport, AirAsia Move, AirAsia Next and Santan. Capital A chief executive officer Tan Sri Tony Fernandes said Effendy's experience and leadership style make him a natural fit for the group's next phase of growth.

items.title: Analysts say Malaysia remains resilient, oil revenues help absorb higher RON95 subsidy bill amid global conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Malaysia remains well-positioned to withstand rising fiscal pressures as the price of Brent crude holds above US$100 per barrel, with stronger petroleum-related revenues expected to cushion the impact of a higher RON95 subsidy bill, said analysts. BMI senior country risk analyst Caroline Wong said higher oil prices would significantly increase the subsidy burden, but elevated crude prices would also lift government revenues, allowing the government to remain broadly on track with its fiscal consolidation plans. The prolonged conflict in West Asia has exerted pressure on global energy markets, with crude oil prices now exceeding US$100 (RM400) per barrel, significantly increasing the country’s subsidy burden.

items.title: [UPDATED] Victor Chin was among 10 detained in Op Viking money laundering sweep
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Businessman Victor Chin Boon Loong is among 10 people detained for alleged involvement in money laundering activities under Op Viking, which was carried out between Oct 15 last year and Jan 13. Bukit Aman Anti-Money Laundering (Amla) investigation team chief Datuk Fazlisyam Abd Majid said Chin was arrested in December last year and later released on police bail. The raids form part of an ongoing money laundering investigation.

items.title: [UPDATED] RM470mil in assets seized in Op Viking money laundering probe
items.summary: The Bukit Aman Anti-Money Laundering (Amla) investigation team said the case, opened in October last year following intelligence gathered since 2018, involved a complex web of offences ranging from illegal moneylending and stock market manipulation to corporate disclosure violations. Bukit Aman Amla chief Datuk Fazlisyam Abd Majid said investigations revealed the group was allegedly involved in offences under the Moneylenders Act 1951, Capital Markets and Services Act 2007, and Companies Act 2016. "Police also seized 2.98 billion units of shares with a market value of RM323.6 million, along with trading accounts worth RM64.2 million and 404 bank accounts totalling RM85.6 million.

items.title: Selangor exco wants maximum jail, caning for fatal drink-driving offences
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A Selangor state executive councillor has called for tougher penalties, including maximum jail terms and caning, for drink-driving offences following a fatal crash in Klang yesterday. State Human Resources and Poverty Eradication Committee chairman V. Papparaidu said existing measures appeared insufficient as a deterrent, citing the rising number of drink-driving cases. Firm action must be prioritised for everyone's safety," he said in a Facebook post.

items.title: 'Bullets fell like rubber leaves': Ex-cop recalls deadly ambush
items.summary: BACHOK: The memory of watching two comrades fall in a hail of bullets remains vivid for former Police Field Force personnel Hassan Jaafar, even after decades. Hassan said the attack unfolded rapidly, with gunfire raining down relentlessly on the team. "The bullets came non-stop, like rubber leaves falling.

items.title: [UPDATED] Glove stocks stage surprise rally, Top Glove leads
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Shares of Bursa Malaysia's top four glove makers staged a surprise rally today, shrugging off a soft start at the opening bell. Top Glove Corp Bhd, which ranked second on Bursa's most active list, rose 10.53 per cent or six sen to 63 sen, with 93.8 million shares traded, its busiest session in more than five months. Earlier, the stock opened 0.88 per cent or half a sen lower at 56.5 sen before climbing 13.27 per cent or 7.5 sen to an intraday high of 64 sen. Hartalega Holdings Bhd followed closely, rising 13 per cent or 13 sen to RM1.13, with 51.93 million shares changing hands, its highest volume in more than a year.

items.title: Cambodia passes tough anti-scam law, life jail for kingpins
items.summary: PHNOM PENH: The National Assembly of Cambodia today passed a draft law on combating online scams, which will deliver up to 30 years or life imprisonment to scam bosses, reported Xinhua. According to the Bill, online scam bosses will face between 15 and 30 years or life imprisonment if their operations lead to one or many deaths. Ringleaders of online scam centres will face five to 10 years' prison and a fine of up to 1 billion riels (RM1 million).

items.title: Report: BYD may rethink Malaysia plans amid Miti’s export, pricing conditions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Chinese electric vehicle (EV) maker BYD may reconsider its plans to set up a completely knocked down (CKD) assembly facility in Tanjong Malim after failing to agree with conditions set by the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (Miti), The Edge reported. Johari was quoted as saying that vehicles sold locally must also be priced above RM200,000, a condition that BYD did not accept. Johari was also quoted as noting that Proton sells about 150,000 units annually, while Perodua records sales of about 350,000 units.

items.title: Guidelines on the way: MPs may soon be barred from stirring 3R issues in Parliament
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The National Unity Ministry is drafting guidelines to prevent Members of Parliament (MPs) from touching on sensitive issues of race, religion and the monarchy (3R) during debates in the Dewan Rakyat. Deputy Minister R. Yuneswaran said the report will be submitted to Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Dr Johari Abdul soon. Yuneswaran also said he supports a proposal by the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia to introduce a National Harmony Act.

items.title: Deadly drink-driving: A look at recent cases
items.summary: Notably, punishment under Section 44(1) for driving under the influence (DUI) of alcohol or drugs causing death or injury now sees first-time offenders facing up to 15 years in jail, a RM100,000 fine, and a 10-year driving ban. The amendments also lowered legal alcohol limits to align with World Health Organization (WHO) standards. The New Straits Times looks back at recent drink-driving cases.

items.title: Eight men plead not guilty in George Town court to charges of injuring three policemen during scuffle
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 30 — Eight men pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate’s Court here today to a charge of intentionally causing injury to three policemen, last Wednesday. Noor Nizar Shah Zainol Abidin, 37; Muhammad Syafiq Mohd Nordin, 28; Azwandy Azmi, 38; Muhamad Aiqal Zulkifrey, 28; Muhammad Firdaus Abdul Malik, 34; Muhammad Razin Abdul Muthalib, 34; Muhammad Syazwan Mohd Omar, 30 and Low Jia Hui, 26, made the plea before Magistrate Tunku Intan Nadiah Tunku Mohammed Nazarin. According to the charge, they were jointly charged with intentionally causing injury to Lance Corporal S. Uvaneand, 26, Constable Muhammad Danial Danish Azlan, 23 and Lance Corporal Muhammad Amzaruddin Fakhri Khuiruddin, 30, while they were carrying out their duties as lawful civil servants.

items.title: In Seremban, babysitter jailed one year, fined RM15,000 over death of ‘Baby Syifaa’ four years ago
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — A babysitter was sentenced to one year’s imprisonment by the Sessions Court in Seremban today for negligence that led to the death of 15-month-old “Baby Syifaa” four years ago. The court also imposed a RM15,000 fine, with an additional four months’ jail in default of payment. Also present in court were Baby Syifaa’s parents, Anida Idrus, 44, and Yuseri Yusoff, 42.

items.title: One man, his dog, and ChatGPT: Australia's AI vaccine saga
items.summary: Sydney-based AI consultant Conyngham told AFP that eight-year-old Rosie's mast cell cancer is now in partial remission and her biggest tumour has shrunk dramatically. Conyngham does not call his findings a cure – but experts unrelated to the dogged endeavour said they highlight AI's potential to accelerate medical research. "I would have conversations and just keep them going non-stop" with ChatGPT, Gemini and Grok to study cancer therapies in-depth, Conyngham said.

items.title: Penang says water supply secure for two months amid dry spell
items.summary: Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said the current reserves in the Muda Dam and Beris Dam in Kedah are sufficient to support water supply through April and May. He said the Muda Dam capacity has dropped to a critical 7.19 per cent as of March 28 and can no longer release water into Sungai Muda after significant drawdowns earlier this year for irrigation. The capacity of the 122,400 ML Beris Dam was at 100 per cent on January 1 and its capacity has dropped to 58.06 per cent as of March 28.

items.title: MMEA rescues 13 tourists after boat stalls at sea
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) rescued 13 tourists after their boat stalled off Kuala Besut yesterday afternoon. State MMEA director Captain Hamiludin Che Awang said the group, which included 12 foreigners, was rescued by an MMEA team on a routine patrol. He said the patrol team spotted the boat, approximately 7.3 nautical miles from the Kuala Besut estuary, drifting, and passengers waving for help.

items.title: KJ's suit against Jamal to be heard in Sessions Court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court here today ruled that former Health Minister Khairy Jamaluddin's application to file a lawsuit against Sungai Besar Umno division chief Jamal Yunos must be heard at the Sessions Court. Judge Datuk Anand Ponnudurai made the decision on the grounds that claims filed in the High Court must involve amounts exceeding RM1 million. Lawyer V. Mugunthan, representing Jamal as the defendant, said his client was prepared to issue an apology, although the plaintiff was also seeking damages.

items.title: Three-week water disruption in Gua Musang’s Rantau Manis forces residents to bathe at R&R and rivers
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, March 30 — Some residents of Taman Agropolitan Rantau Manis here have been forced to bathe at highway rest and service areas (R&R), rivers, and transport water from the town located about 40 kilometres away due to the water supply disruption that has persisted for three weeks. Sahazudin Mohammad, 52, said he had to rely on R&R facilities to bathe despite living in his own home. “This water supply disruption has made daily life difficult for the residents, especially during Ramadan and the Aidilfitri celebrations.

items.title: World Bank Group's IFC, OCI TerraSus team up to advance Malaysia's semiconductor push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The World Bank Group, via its private-sector arm the International Finance Corporation (IFC), has partnered with OCI TerraSus Sdn Bhd (OCI TRS), a unit of South Korea's OCI Holdings Company Ltd, to advance Malaysia's participation in the global semiconductor supply chain and create jobs in the country. They said the partnership would also drive enhancements in IFC's environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards and management systems. OCI Holdings and OCI TerraSus chairman Lee Woo Hyun said the partnership is a key driver in strengthening management systems, ESG practices and long-term competitiveness as a global company.

items.title: Philippine peso hits record low, Indonesian rupiah steadies after support steps
items.summary: BENGALURU: The Philippine peso hit a record low on Monday and the Indonesian rupiah stopped short of touching a historic trough of 17,000 per dollar as the country's central bank introduced a new tool for banks to manage foreign-currency liquidity. The Philippine peso hit a low of 60.776 per dollar, while stocks in Manila tumbled 2.5 per cent, as the net oil importergrapples with an "imminent danger of a critically low energy supply." Bank Indonesia on Monday implemented new foreign exchange repo transactions using its FX-denominated securities, providing banks with an alternative for liquidity management, particularly foreign exchange liquidity.

items.title: Caretaker jailed over neglect linked to Baby Syifaa's death
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A caretaker was sentenced to one year's imprisonment by the Sessions Court here today for neglect that led to the death of 15-month-old Nour Rania Asyifaa Yuseri, known as Baby Syifaa, four years ago. The court also imposed a RM15,000 fine, with an additional four months' imprisonment in default. Baby Syifaa's parents, Anida Idrus, 44, and Yuseri Yusoff, 42, were present in court.

items.title: Farmers’ organisation board staff pleads not guilty to RM141,872 embezzlement in Johor Bahru
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 30 — A 36-year-old staff of the Farmers’ Organisation Board (LPP) claimed trial in the Sessions Court here today to 16 charges of fraudulently misappropriating property belonging to the Kota Tinggi Timur Farmers’ Organisation totalling RM141,872.67 over the past five years. Based on the charge sheets, the accused, who is also the Kota Tinggi Timur Area Farmers’ Organisation’s economic affairs assistant officer and manager, is charged with fraudulently embezzling proceeds from the sale of palm oil Fresh Fruit Bunches (FFB) totalling RM141,872.67 that belonged to the organisation. The accused was charged under Section 403 of the Penal Code for fraudulent misappropriation of property which carries a maximum prison sentence of five years, whipping and also a fine upon conviction.

items.title: Selangor exco calls for maximum jail and caning for fatal drink-driving cases
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 30 — A Selangor state executive councillor has called for tougher penalties, including maximum imprisonment and caning, for drink-driving offences following a fatal crash in Klang yesterday. State Human Resources and Poverty Eradication Committee chairman V. Papparaidu said existing measures appeared insufficient as a deterrent, as evidenced by the rising number of drink-driving cases. Yesterday, Klang Selatan district police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said a motorcyclist was killed after being hit by a car driven by an individual under the influence of alcohol and drugs on Jalan Raya Barat.

items.title: Ex-Army chief Hafizuddeain and wife to face joint trial over illegal proceeds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The 11-count case of receiving money from illegal activities involving former Army Chief (PTD) Tan Sri Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan and his wife, Salwani Anuar @ Kamaruddin will be tried together at the Sessions Court here. Earlier, Mahadi said Muhammad Hafizuddeain was previously charged at the Shah Alam Sessions Court and the case was transferred to the Sessions Court here, while Salwani was charged at the Kuala Terengganu Sessions Court and the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court. On January 22, Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan was charged at the Sessions Court here on four counts of receiving proceeds of illegal activities, namely money amounting to RM969,000, RM474,850, RM488,550 and RM190,000 which were deposited into three bank accounts belonging to him.

items.title: Malaysians in Qatar urged to follow safety rules as schools reopen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Malaysian citizens in Qatar are advised to always comply with the safety instructions and guidelines issued by Qatari authorities following the reopening of schools in the country. “With the reopening of schools, all Malaysians in Qatar are advised to constantly comply with the instructions and safety guidelines set by the Qatari authorities,” according to a statement uploaded on the Embassy’s Facebook page on Sunday. Qatar’s Ministry of Education and Higher Education announced last week the gradual resumption of in-person classes across educational institutions in the country starting March 24, with full in-person attendance for all students in schools and kindergartens resuming on March 29, Qatar News Agency (QNA) reported.

items.title: Penang courts tourists from China, India, Asean to offset Middle East and European shortfall
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 30 — Penang is looking to attract more tourists from China, India and neighbouring countries in South-east Asia to make up for the shortfall in visitors from the Middle East and European countries. Penang Tourism and Creative Economy Committee chairman Wong Hon Wai said currently, two airlines are still grounded in Doha and Dubai due to the conflict in Middle East so it will affect tourist arrivals from the Middle East as well as Europeans. He said there are increasing direct flights from China such as the inaugural flight from Qingdao Airline from northern China.

items.title: Federal Court decision could determine future of Shah Alam Community Forest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The fate of the Shah Alam Community Forest (SACF) is set to be decided by the Federal Court, which will rule on a legal challenge over its status as part of a forest reserve. According to The Star , the decision could directly affect a contentious road project cutting through the SACF, which has faced strong opposition from residents and environmental groups. Selangor public health and environment committee chairman Jamaliah Jamaluddin said the state government had yet to take an official position, with the matter expected to be discussed at the next state executive council (MMKN) meeting.

items.title: King receives update on Online Safety Act to shield children from social media risks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the King of Malaysia, was today presented with the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA 2025) during a royal audience at Istana Bukit Tunku, according to a post on the King’s official Facebook page. Among its key measures, the act introduces clear responsibilities for online platforms and implements an age verification mechanism to prevent children under 16 from opening social media accounts, safeguarding their safety online. Fahmi also highlighted that ONSA 2025 seeks to address fake accounts targeting the Malay Rulers and royal family members.

items.title: Driver in his 20s remanded four days after fatal Klang crash hurls motorcyclist onto car roof
items.summary: KLANG, March 30 — A man has been remanded for four days from today for allegedly driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs and causing the death of a motorcyclist on Jalan Raya Barat here yesterday. Yesterday, Klang Selatan Police chief ACP Lim Jit Huey said the incident, which occurred at about 11.40am, caused the victim, a man in his 30s, to be flung onto the roof of another car. He said initial investigations found that a Honda City driven by a man heading towards Klang town was being dangerously driven at high speed.

items.title: King meets PM Anwar to discuss federal administration, current issues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the King of Malaysia, today received Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim in a formal audience at Istana Bukit Tunku. According to a post on the King’s official Facebook page, the meeting included updates on the federal government’s administration and discussions on current issues. The audience was held at the palace earlier today (March 30).

items.title: Massive 5.5m python caught feasting on calf in Baling oil palm plantation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — A 5.5-metre python was spotted swallowing a calf at a palm oil plantation near Paya Keladi in Baling, Kedah, yesterday. According to the owner, Wan Hasbullah Wan Ismail, 57, the calf went missing in the afternoon while grazing with its mother in the area. Baling Civil Defence officer Leftenan (PA) Mohd Faizol Abd Aziz said the team received the call at 8.20pm.
